description: PocketPal mobile inference baselines — Q4_K_M GGUF on-device benchmarks. Used as reference for SRD pack comparisons.

baselines.notes: Baseline for MiniSRDAgent capsule size class (params_m=135). TG limited by OpenCL pipeline underutilisation at 135M scale.

ppl.note: Error bars overlap with F16 — delta is at noise floor on 100 chunks

baselines.notes: Primary SRD benchmark target. TG 2.5x faster than 135M despite 8x more params — OpenCL saturates better at 1B scale.

results.note: Speed not benchmarked on device. Estimated TG ~3-4 t/s based on 3.3x bandwidth vs Q4_K_M.

ppl.note: Reference baseline. v1.0 chat PPL ~19.2 vs v0.6 chat ~9.88 — v1.0 is chat-aligned, worse at raw text continuation.

baselines.notes: F16 PPL reference only — no mobile speed run. Use Q4_K_M for deployment: 3.5x smaller, 1.5x faster TG, <1% quality loss.

srd_targets.description: Expected SRD-4 results vs Q4_K_M baselines. NOTE: the '1.51 PPL improvement' claim in docs/SRD_RESULTS.md used cited K-quant numbers measured on the BASE model with a different stride. The measured Q4_K_M vs F16 gap on the v1.0 chat model is only +0.94% (statistically noise). SRD α=0 advantage over Q4_K_M must be re-measured with the same methodology before citing.

tinyllama_1b.ppl_improvement_vs_q4km_status: NEEDS_RECHECK — original claim used base model cited numbers; measured Q4_K_M gap to F16 is only 0.18 PPL (0.94%) on v1.0 chat
tinyllama_1b.action: Re-run SRD α=0 PPL measurement against Q4_K_M on same model/dataset/stride to get a fair delta

key_findings.q4km_vs_f16_actual: Q4_K_M loses only 0.18 PPL (0.94%) vs F16 on TinyLlama-1.1B v1.0 chat — error bars overlap, difference is statistical noise on 100 chunks. 3.5x size reduction for essentially zero quality loss.
key_findings.v10_vs_v06: v1.0 chat PPL ~19.2, v0.6 chat PPL ~9.88 on wikitext-2. v1.0 is more chat-aligned but worse at raw text completion — expected for instruction-tuned models.
key_findings.srd_claim_status: The +1.51 PPL SRD vs Q4_K_M claim in docs/SRD_RESULTS.md was based on cited K-quant numbers from the base model with stride mismatch. Real measured Q4_K_M gap to F16 is much smaller. SRD advantage needs re-measurement on the same setup.
